content.full_text:
Memorandum

Advice No. M-83-054

March 2, 1983

TO: Advice Request Meeting Participants
FROM: Alice Hughes
RE: Advice to City Attorney of Mountain View

The City Attorney for Mountain View, Pete Bilens, asked the following question:

A city councilman who is an attorney specializing in tax and business law set up a corporation which was wholly owned by family members. The corporation was only established in order to borrow money from family members and loan it to other family members. Once the money was borrowed or lent, the corporation was dissolved. (This occurs again and again.) Does he have to report the corporation on his SEI as an investment? Are the loan(s) that were received by the corporation from family members reportable?

AH:km

March 10, 1983

2/3/83 meeting:

He should report the investment in the corporations. The value of the corporation is the highest amount of money it holds during the reporting period. He does not need to report the loans. (This assumes he and his family own 100% of the corporations.)
